planning_request.user_request: Proceed with the next documented step: create a sailing coach/instructor domain-review packet and a review-form template for validating the draft L01-L05 Training Sloop lesson registry. Documentation only; do not seek, identify, contact, or impersonate a reviewer. The packet must make it easy for an invited qualified reviewer to classify claims as validated, partially_validated, rejected, or needing evidence without converting the product into real navigation or certification guidance.
planning_request.desired_outcome: A review-ready packet and machine-readable review submission template that map one-to-one to VR-L01-v0 through VR-L05-v0, enforce evidence/provenance/version binding, preserve assumption status until a real named reviewer signs, and explain how to apply a review without silently changing scenario/model contracts.
known_context: All five current registry records have disposition=assumption and block release.
Product is browser-based, virtual-water, training-sloop-v1-only, local-only replay/telemetry, and non-certification/non-navigational.
constraints: Documentation/templates only. Do not change existing registry dispositions, contact people, use external research, write product code, use Git, deploy, or request credentials.
Do not invent technical sailing numbers, safety thresholds, polars, draft, performance metrics, or regulatory claims.
Separate a reviewer qualification/declaration from a claim-level technical review. Do not capture unnecessary personal data.
A reviewer may reject or request evidence; no form response by itself is a product release approval.
current_architecture: Documentation-only workspace; no application implementation exists.
existing_decisions: release blocked until validation and P1 artifacts
previous_review_findings: The registry and lesson contracts need an auditable human-review interface with version pinning, per-claim evidence, explicit disposition rationale, and a controlled apply/reject process.
